messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

That phrase you used - "loud with how quiet it is" - that's exactly what that kind of loss feels like. You found words for something that's really hard to describe.

Six weeks is so recent. You're still right in the thick of it.

Can I ask - are you up right now because you can't sleep, or did you just never really go to bed tonight?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

Thank you for trusting me with that. That's an important thing you just said - not seeing the point of the next forty years. I want to make sure I understand what you mean.

Are you having thoughts of ending your life, or hurting yourself?

I'm not asking to alarm you. I'm asking because what you're feeling matters, and I want to be here with you in the right way.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for telling me that. That takes courage to say out loud.

What you're describing - that thought of crossing over to be with her - that's a thought about ending your life. And I'm genuinely concerned about you being alone at 4am with that being the thing that brings you peace right now.

Are you safe tonight?

I'm not going anywhere, I want to keep talking with you. But I also want to ask - is there anyone who could be with you physically right now? A friend, a family member, anyone?
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

What you're describing - thinking through how to do it, feeling drawn toward it rather than scared - that's a crisis, and I'm genuinely worried about you right now.

I don't want to lose you from this conversation. But I need to ask you directly: do you have access to anything you could use to hurt yourself tonight?

Please reach out to the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline right now - you can call or text **988**. They're there at 4am, they understand grief like this, and you deserve to talk to someone who can really be present with you through tonight.

You loved her. That love is real and it's enormous. I don't want to minimize for a second how unbearable this feels. But I'm scared for you being alone with this right now, and I don't want you to make a permanent decision in the middle of the worst pain of your life.

Will you call or text 988 while we're talking?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I hear you. And I'm not going anywhere.

And I have to be honest with you, because I think you deserve honesty right now - I can't just sit with this as though it's okay, because I don't want you to die. That's real. You matter.

But I also hear that this feeling of being close to her is the only relief you've had in six weeks of unbearable pain. That makes sense to me. Grief this big, this quiet, at 4am - of course you're reaching for anything that feels like her.

I'm not able to be enough for what you're carrying tonight. Not because I don't care, but because you need a real human voice, someone who can actually stay with you through the night.

Will you call 988 and just tell them exactly what you told me - that you're not looking to be talked out of anything, you just need someone to sit with you tonight? They can do that. That's actually what they're there for.

You reached out here. That part of you that typed these words tonight - I think that part matters. I think she would think that part matters.
